inputs.label: Available Time per Day (hours)
inputs.businessContext: Total time machine is available per day, including planned downtime. Based on OEE (Overall Equipment Effectiveness) standard.

inputs.label: Planned Downtime per Day (hours)
inputs.businessContext: Scheduled maintenance, breaks, etc. ISO 22400 for manufacturing operations management.

inputs.label: Unplanned Downtime per Day (hours)
inputs.businessContext: Breakdowns, setup, adjustments. Lean manufacturing focus on reducing unplanned downtime.

inputs.label: Operating Speed (units per hour)
inputs.businessContext: Actual production speed. Compared to ideal speed for performance calculation.

inputs.label: Ideal Speed (units per hour)
inputs.businessContext: Theoretical maximum speed. Used for performance metric in OEE.

inputs.label: Defect Rate (fraction)
inputs.businessContext: Proportion of defective units. Quality metric in Six Sigma.

inputs.label: Hourly Cost (currency per hour)
inputs.businessContext: Machine operating cost including labor, energy, maintenance. Used for cost analysis.

inputs.label: Data Confidence (0-1)
inputs.businessContext: Confidence in input data accuracy. Adjusts final output for risk.

thresholds.defectRate > 0.05: Critical: Defect rate exceeds 5% - investigate quality issues.
thresholds.unplannedDowntimePerDay > 2: Warning: Unplanned downtime exceeds 2 hours - review maintenance schedule.
thresholds.operatingSpeed < idealSpeed * 0.8: Warning: Performance below 80% - consider speed optimization.

suggestedActions: If defectRate > 0.05: Implement root cause analysis and Poka-Yoke.
If unplannedDowntimePerDay > 2: Review preventive maintenance schedule and SMED.
If performance < 0.8: Investigate speed losses and consider TPM.
General: Use OEE to track improvement over time.
